part.text: I detect investigation/review intent - you want read-only correctness findings, not implementation. My approach: parallel codebase exploration for validation/scoring assumptions, then synthesize concise severity-ranked bugs without editing.

input.prompt:
1. TASK: Review the KMU RecSys Steam validation split construction scripts for correctness bugs only.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise findings with severity focused on data leakage, train/validation candidate construction, per-user positive/negative counts, and whether fold-train/candidate overlap checks are sufficient. Include exact file/line references where possible.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: Read-only code inspection tools only (read, grep, glob). Do not edit files, run destructive commands, or modify artifacts.
4. MUST DO: Inspect scripts/build_validation_splits.py and scripts/recsys_played_utils.py. Pay special attention to use of full history vs fold train, item universe selection, candidate IDs, skipped/adjusted users, negative sampling, and top-half target count logic.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not run Kaggle submission or write outputs. Do not make style suggestions; correctness bugs only.
6. CONTEXT: User asked: "Review these KMU RecSys Steam validation/scoring scripts for correctness bugs only. Do not edit files. Focus on leakage, per-user top-half evaluation, split construction, EASE formula, and baseline reproduction." The already-read files are scripts/recsys_played_utils.py and scripts/build_validation_splits.py.

input.prompt:
1. TASK: Review the KMU RecSys Steam scoring/evaluation scripts for correctness bugs only.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise findings with severity focused on per-user top-half evaluation, EASE formula correctness, popularity/itemKNN scoring assumptions, and README baseline reproduction. Include exact file/line references where possible.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: Read-only code inspection tools only (read, grep, glob). Do not edit files, run destructive commands, or modify artifacts.
4. MUST DO: Inspect scripts/score_popularity_itemknn_ease.py, scripts/evaluate_tophalf.py, and scripts/recsys_played_utils.py. Verify top-half sorting/tie-breaking, Label handling for validation vs test, EASE B formula orientation/sign, and README popularity baseline reproduction details.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not run Kaggle submission or write outputs. Do not make style suggestions; correctness bugs only.
6. CONTEXT: User asked: "Review these KMU RecSys Steam validation/scoring scripts for correctness bugs only. Do not edit files. Focus on leakage, per-user top-half evaluation, split construction, EASE formula, and baseline reproduction." The already-read files are scripts/recsys_played_utils.py, scripts/score_popularity_itemknn_ease.py, and scripts/evaluate_tophalf.py.
